treatment_information.dosage_or_details: titrated from 7.5 mg orally/week to 15 mg orally/week over 1 month
treatment_information.treatment_response: skin lesions significantly improved, less joint pain, stiffness and itching, but still experienced shoulder pain and morning stiffness
skin lesions significantly improved, less joint pain, stiffness and itching, but still experienced shoulder pain and morning stiffness
patient improved and was completely weaned off steroids over a 2-month period; however, he continued to feel very stiff and have arthralgias, with synovitis observed in his hands and shoulders

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 50 mg orally two times a day
treatment_information.treatment_response: cutaneous manifestations remained quiet, arthralgias improved

treatment_timeline.event: Started methotrexate (7.5 mg orally/week) and etanercept (50 mg/week, subcutaneous)
Methotrexate dosage increased to 15 mg orally/week over 1 month
Patient placed on prednisone (5 mg orally daily)
Etanercept replaced with adalimumab (40 mg every 2 weeks, subcutaneous)
Minocycline (50 mg orally two times a day) added to treatment regimen
Patient weaned off steroids over a 2-month period
Patient remained on methotrexate, adalimumab and minocycline for the last 3 years

patient_outcome.final_status: cutaneous manifestations remained quiet, arthralgias improved
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient did not want to resume steroids or increase his methotrexate as he was concerned about the potential side effects
